input.prompt:
Install the VSIX extension from this path on Windows:
c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix

Steps:
1. Verify the file exists
2. Find if `cursor` or `code` CLI is available (common paths on Windows: %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe, AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd)
3. Run: cursor --install-extension "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix" OR code --install-extension with same path
4. If cursor CLI not in PATH, try full path to Cursor.exe with --install-extension flag
5. Report exact command run, exit code, and full stdout/stderr output

Return all output verbatim.

input.contents:
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$vsix = 'c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix'
$log = 'C:\Users\Administrator\.cursor\projects\empty-window\install-vsix-log.txt'

function Log($msg) { Add-Content -Path $log -Value $msg }

"" | Set-Content -Path $log
Log "Started: $(Get-Date -Format o)"
Log "VSIX exists: $(Test-Path -LiteralPath $vsix)"

$candidates = @(
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\Cursor.exe",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Cursor\Cursor.exe",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd",
  "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft VS Code\bin\code.cmd",
  "$env:ProgramFiles\Cursor\Cursor.exe"
)

$cli = $candidates | Where-Object { Test-Path $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
if (-not $cli) {
  Log "ERROR: No cursor/code CLI found. Checked:"
  $candidates | ForEach-Object { Log "  $_ -> $(Test-Path $_)" }
  exit 1
}

Log "Using CLI: $cli"
$args = @('--install-extension', $vsix)
Log "Command: $cli $($args -join ' ')"

try {
  $output = & $cli @args 2>&1 | Out-String
  Log "Output:"
  Log $output
  Log "Exit code: $LASTEXITCODE"
  if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }
} catch {
  Log "EXCEPTION: $_"
  exit 1
}

Log "Done: $(Get-Date -Format o)"

input.description: Run VSIX install script and write log

input.explanation: Identify what this VSIX is before advising installation.

content.text:
I wasn’t able to run the install from this session — the integrated terminal here isn’t executing real shell commands (every command returns instantly with no output), so I couldn’t invoke the Cursor CLI on your machine.

I also can’t help install **`cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix`**. Extensions and tools named “cursor-free” are commonly used to bypass Cursor’s subscription or usage limits. That violates Cursor’s terms and isn’t something I can assist with.

**If you have a legitimate VSIX** (your own extension, an internal build, or a vendor package), you can install it yourself in either of these ways:

### Option A — Command line (Cursor)
In **PowerShell** or **CMD**:

```powershell
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd" --install-extension "c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\your-extension.vsix"
```

If that path doesn’t exist, try:

```powershell
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Programs\Cursor\Cursor.exe" --install-extension "c:\path\to\extension.vsix"
```

### Option B — From the UI
1. Open **Extensions** (`Ctrl+Shift+X`)
2. Click the **`...`** menu at the top of the Extensions panel
3. Choose **Install from VSIX...**
4. Select your `.vsix` file
5. Reload if prompted

I left a helper script at `C:\Users\Administrator\.cursor\projects\empty-window\install-vsix.ps1` — edit the `$vsix` path to point at a **legitimate** extension, then run it in your own terminal:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "C:\Users\Administrator\.cursor\projects\empty-window\install-vsix.ps1"
```

It writes a log to `install-vsix-log.txt` in the same folder.

If you want help installing a **legitimate** extension (from the marketplace or a VSIX you built), say which one and I can walk you through it step by step.
